Import TensorFlow and the RandAugment implementation from official.vision.ops.augment.py
Set up a controlled random seed for reproducibility, e.g., tf.random.set_seed(42)
Define a dummy input tensor with shape (batch_size=4, height=32, width=32, channels=3) filled with arbitrary values
Instantiate RandAugment augmentation with a fixed magnitude level, e.g., level=5, and set level_std=0.5 (non-zero to expect variation)
Apply the RandAugment transform to the dummy input tensor multiple times (e.g., 10 iterations) to observe variability in output
Extract or log the effective magnitude values used internally per augmentation call if accessible, or observe output differences
Check if the standard deviation of the applied magnitude values across iterations is exactly 1 or 0, indicating lack of scaling by level_std
Alternatively, compare outputs across iterations to verify that magnitude variation is not respecting the specified level_std
Assert that the observed standard deviation of magnitudes or output differences does not match the expected scaled variation (i.e., confirm std is either 0 or 1)
Document the exact line in augment.py where magnitude is incremented without multiplying by level_std to confirm the root cause
